infobox.value: ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): بحمرة, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Arabic "Romanization of Arabic"): *Bahāmra*; also spelled *Bhamrah*) is a village in northwestern [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), administratively part of the [Qardaha District](/wiki/Qardaha_District "Qardaha District") of the [Latakia Governorate](/wiki/Latakia_Governorate "Latakia Governorate"). It is located near [Qardaha](/wiki/Qardaha "Qardaha") and 29 kilometers (18 mi) east of [Latakia](/wiki/Latakia "Latakia"). According to the [Syria Central Bureau of Statistics](/wiki/Central_Bureau_of_Statistics_(Syria) "Central Bureau of Statistics (Syria)"), Bahamra had a population of 1,028 in the 2004 census.[[1]](#cite_note-CBS-1) The inhabitants of the village are [Alawites](/wiki/Alawites "Alawites").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Village in Latakia, Syria

| Bahamra  بحمرة  Bhamrah | |

| • Summer ([DST](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")) | [UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B3 "UTC+3") ([EEST](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time")) |

**Bahamra** ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): بحمرة, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Arabic "Romanization of Arabic"): *Bahāmra*; also spelled *Bhamrah*) is a village in northwestern [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), administratively part of the [Qardaha District](/wiki/Qardaha_District "Qardaha District") of the [Latakia Governorate](/wiki/Latakia_Governorate "Latakia Governorate"). It is located near [Qardaha](/wiki/Qardaha "Qardaha") and 29 kilometers (18 mi) east of [Latakia](/wiki/Latakia "Latakia"). According to the [Syria Central Bureau of Statistics](/wiki/Central_Bureau_of_Statistics_(Syria) "Central Bureau of Statistics (Syria)"), Bahamra had a population of 1,028 in the 2004 census.[[1]](#cite_note-CBS-1) The inhabitants of the village are [Alawites](/wiki/Alawites "Alawites").

The British [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") missionary [Samuel Lyde](/wiki/Samuel_Lyde "Samuel Lyde") based his mission in Syria at Bahamra in 1854–1859.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWinter2016121-2) He purchased property, opened his school and put up a number of other buildings there and attempted to proselytize mainly among the Alawites of the region.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBalph191335-3) He did not meet much success in his endeavour and transferred the school to the American [Presbyterian](/wiki/Presbyterian "Presbyterian") missionary R. J. Dobbs, who continued the mission's work.[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBalph191336-4) At some point in the 1860s the school was closed, but the Protestant boys' school that had been opened in Latakia was transferred to Bahamra in 1870, where it operated until 1876.[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBalph191347-5) Missionary work continued to be carried out from the village at least until 1913.[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBalph1913146-6)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bahamra&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Ghazi Kanaan](/wiki/Ghazi_Kanaan "Ghazi Kanaan"), head of Syrian intelligence services in Lebanon in 1982–2002.[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEVloeberghs201680,_note_176-7)
